description: This figure is a force-deflection hysteresis diagram for φ50 mm Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steel specimens subjected to elasto-plastic cyclic bending tests. The x-axis represents deflection (H, unit: mm, range from -200 to +200 mm), while the y-axis represents applied load (Q, unit: ×10³ N, range from -50×10³ N to +50×10³ N). Three curves are plotted, corresponding to the 1st loading cycle, 2nd cycle, and 12th (last) cycle of the test. The hysteresis loop shape shows minimal change across increasing cycle counts, indicating the work spent on elastoplastic deformation per cycle remains nearly constant during repeated loading. This stable cyclic behavior demonstrates that Fe-Mn-Al-C steel has excellent elasto-plastic fatigue resistance: unfailed specimens of this alloy can sustain 4 times more loading cycles than comparative Fe-13Cr-0.2C martensitic chromium steel under identical test conditions, supporting its suitability for components under cyclic loading service conditions.
